title: A Handbook of Marxism
description: A Handbook of Marxism is a non-fiction by Emile Burns. Banned by 1935.
descriptionParagraphs: A Handbook of Marxism is a non-fiction by Emile Burns. Banned by 1935.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a non-fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned by 1935. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: A Handbook of Marxism is a non-fiction by Emile Burns. Banned by 1935.
banningParagraphs: A Handbook of Marxism entered censorship debates as a non-fiction associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.
The earliest event currently captured here is 20th century in New Zealand, where Customs Department classified, prohibited, or restricted. Banned by 1935. Banned by 1935.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.
